articles.title: Is the US government’s Anthropic ban accidentally helping the brand?
articles.subtitle: Just as last week was ending, the US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5’s guardrails. Cybersecu
articles.author: Theresa Loconsolo
articles.summary: Just as last week was ending, the US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5’s guardrails. Cybersecurity researchers have since signed an open letter calling the move dangerous, and Anthropic itself noted the same jailbreaks exist in other models. So is […]
body: Loading the player… var playerInstance_31346081 = jwplayer( "jwppp-video-31346081" ); playerInstance_31346081.setup({ playlist: "https://cdn.jwplayer.com/v2/media/UdkrWKnw", })

Just as last week was ending, the US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models , Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5’s guardrails.

Cybersecurity researchers have since signed an open letter calling the move dangerous, and Anthropic itself noted the same jailbreaks exist in other models. So is this a genuine security concern, or just the latest chapter in a messy relationship between Anthropic and the Trump administration?

On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, hosts Anthony Ha, Sean O’Kane, and Rebecca Bellan unpack what the ban means for developers building on Anthropic’s platform and for anyone watching the IPO, why it might accidentally be good for the company , and more of the week’s headlines.
Subscribe to Equity on YouTube , Apple Podcasts , Overcast , Spotify and all the casts. You also can follow Equity on X and Threads , at @EquityPod.

Get an inside look at what it takes to scale and succeed from leaders at Mach Industries, Founders Fund, and Shinkei Systems. Through candid fireside chats and high-impact networking, you’ll walk away with valuable insights and new connections.

The US government’s Anthropic models ban was never about an AI jailbreak Zack Whittaker
As Anthropic suspends access to new models, India debates its AI future Jagmeet Singh
Amazon CEO reportedly raised Anthropic model concerns before government crackdown Anthony Ha

Just as last week was ending, the US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models , Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5’s guardrails.

Cybersecurity researchers have since signed an open letter calling the move dangerous, and Anthropic itself noted the same jailbreaks exist in other models. So is this a genuine security concern, or just the latest chapter in a messy relationship between Anthropic and the Trump administration?
paragraphTranslations: 加载播放器… var playerInstance_31346081 = jwplayer( "jwppp-video-31346081" ); playerInstance_31346081.setup({ playlist: "https://cdn.jwplayer.com/v2/media/UdkrWKnw", })
就在上周即将结束时，美国政府以国家安全为由，迫使Anthropic撤回其两款最新型号Fable 5和Mythos 5，此前亚马逊研究人员声称找到了绕过Fable 5安全防护的方法。
网络安全研究人员随后签署了一封公开信，称此举危险，Anthropic本身也指出其他型号存在相同的越狱行为。那么，这是一个真正的安全问题，还是Anthropic与特朗普政府之间混乱关系的最新篇章呢？
在这一期的TechCrunch股权播客中，主持人安东尼·哈、肖恩·奥凯恩和丽贝卡·贝兰解析了禁令对在Anthropic平台上构建的开发者以及任何关注IPO的人意味着什么，为什么它可能无意中对公司有好处，以及本周的更多头条新闻。
在YouTube、Apple Podcasts、Overcast、Spotify以及所有播客平台上订阅Equity。你还可以在X和Threads上关注Equity，@EquityPod。
深入了解Mach Industries、Founders Fund和Shinkei Systems的领导者们是如何扩大规模并取得成功的。通过坦率的炉边谈话和高影响力的网络连接，你将获得宝贵的见解和新的联系。

SpaceX以6000亿美元的股票收购Cursor，继重磅IPO几天后 肖恩·奥凯恩
美国政府禁止Anthropic模型从未与AI越狱有关 扎克·惠特克

paragraphs: The US government recently forced Anthropic to withdraw its two latest AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, due to national security concerns. This decision came after Amazon researchers claimed to have found a way to bypass Fable 5’s safety measures. Despite cybersecurity researchers signing an open letter against the ban and Anthropic pointing out that similar vulnerabilities exist in other models, the move has sparked debate on whether it is a genuine security issue or a reflection of Anthropic's strained relationship with the Trump administration.
paragraphTranslations: 美国政府最近强迫Anthropic撤回其两个最新的人工智能模型，Fable 5和Mythos 5，原因是国家安全问题。这一决定是在亚马逊研究人员声称找到了绕过Fable 5安全措施的方法之后做出的。尽管网络安全研究人员签署了一封反对禁令的公开信，Anthropic也指出其他模型中存在类似的漏洞，但此举引发了关于这是否是一个真正的安全问题，还是Anthropic与特朗普政府紧张关系的一个反映的辩论。

articles.title: NASA selects Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for a 2028 mission to Mars
articles.subtitle: Relativity Space, the rocket company led by former Google executive Eric Schmidt, was picked to launch NASA's Aeolus payload to Mars in 2028, as reported earlier by TechCrunch. Under a new public-private partnership, Relativity Space will p

Under a new public-private partnership, Relativity Space will provide the "spacecraft, rocket, and cruise operations" to fly Aeolus to Mars, where the payload will "provide the first […]

﻿Relativity Space will launch NASA’s Aeolus payload, which will gather data on the Martian atmosphere.
﻿Relativity Space will launch NASA’s Aeolus payload, which will gather data on the Martian atmosphere.
Posts from this author will be added to your daily email digest and your homepage feed.
Posts from this author will be added to your daily email digest and your homepage feed.

Relativity Space, the rocket company led by former Google executive Eric Schmidt, was picked to launch NASA’s Aeolus payload to Mars in 2028, as reported earlier by TechCrunch . Under a new public-private partnership, Relativity Space will provide the “spacecraft, rocket, and cruise operations” to fly Aeolus to Mars, where the payload will “provide the first integrated, daily, global view of Martian winds, temperatures, dust, and clouds.”

The Aeolus payload will have four instruments on board for studying the Martian atmosphere, which NASA says will “directly inform entry, descent, and landing systems and support safer, more predictable mission planning for astronauts.”

Schmidt, who served as CEO of Google from 2001 to 2011, became Relativity Space’s CEO in 2025, a couple of years after it launched the “world’s first 3D-printed rocket,” Terran 1, which failed shortly after launch. Relativity Space’s larger Terran R rocket isn’t scheduled to have its first launch until later this year.

﻿相对论空间公司将发射美国宇航局的Aeolus载荷，该载荷将收集有关火星大气的数据。
﻿相对论空间公司将发射美国宇航局的Aeolus载荷，该载荷将收集有关火星大气的数据。
来自该作者的帖子将被添加到您的每日电子邮件摘要和您的主页推送中。
来自该作者的帖子将被添加到您的每日电子邮件摘要和您的主页推送中。
相对论空间公司，这家由前谷歌高管埃里克·施密特领导的火箭公司，被选中在2028年发射美国宇航局的Aeolus载荷到火星，正如TechCrunch之前报道的那样。在一项新的公私合作伙伴关系下，相对论空间公司将提供“航天器、火箭和巡航操作”以将Aeolus送至火星，在那里该载荷将“提供火星风、温度、尘埃和云层的第一个综合、每日、全球视角”。
Aeolus载荷将搭载四个仪器，用于研究火星大气，美国宇航局表示，这将“直接通知进入、下降和着陆系统，并支持宇航员的安全、更可预测的任务规划”。
施密特曾在2001年至2011年担任谷歌的首席执行官，于2025年成为相对论空间公司的首席执行官，这是在公司发射了“世界上第一个3D打印火箭”Terran 1几年后的事情，Terran 1在发射后不久就失败了。相对论空间公司更大的Terran R火箭计划在今年晚些时候进行首次发射。
来自该作者的帖子将被添加到您的每日电子邮件摘要和您的主页推送中。

paragraphs: NASA has chosen Relativity Space, a rocket company led by former Google CEO Eric Schmidt, to launch its Aeolus payload to Mars in 2028. The mission aims to collect data on Martian winds, temperatures, dust, and clouds, providing a daily global view of the Martian atmosphere. This information will help plan safer and more predictable astronaut missions. Aeolus will carry four instruments for studying the atmosphere. Relativity Space, known for launching the world's first 3D-printed rocket, Terran 1, will provide the spacecraft, rocket, and operations for the mission.
paragraphTranslations: 美国宇航局选择了由前谷歌首席执行官埃里克·施密特领导的火箭公司相对论空间，于2028年发射其Aeolus载荷到火星。该任务旨在收集有关火星风、温度、尘埃和云的数据，提供火星大气的每日全球视图。这些信息将有助于规划更安全、更可预测的宇航员任务。Aeolus将携带四个研究大气的仪器。相对论空间以发射世界上第一个3D打印火箭Terran 1而闻名，将为该任务提供航天器、火箭和运营服务。
